name: Ray Bryant
abstract: Raphael Homer "Ray" Bryant (December 24, 1931 – June 2, 2011) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger.

interviews.title: Ray Bryant interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Lionel Hampton
Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Billie Holiday
Newport Jazz Festival: We Remember Billie
Illinois Jacquet: "Jammin' the Blues" 50th Anniversary Celebration

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: it features ten piano players — Hank Jones, John Lewis, Kenny Barron, Ray Bryant, Gene Harris, Joanne Brackeen, Roger Kellaway, and who have I left out, oh, Cedar Walton and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I had Ray Bryant and his brother... One of the finest records... is the one that you made with Ray Bryant and his brother Tommy Bryant and you, called Jo Jones Plus Two.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I'm doing one album coming up that I'm going to be doing with Ray Bryant -- it's just a quartet album

about_quotes.speaker: Mickey Roker
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Gigi Gryce for a couple of years, played with Ray Bryant for a couple of years
about_quotes.doc_title: Mickey Roker interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: So all those things you like that we did, the “Left Bank Collection” and the Ray Bryant stuff... And I can talk about these records and take myself out of the mix. I had nothing to do with the recording. I put them together
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: this writer, this columnist who was involved with the Boys Town of Italy gave Ella’s show a write-up of four columns, twelve paragraphs, about Ray Bryant, who was the piano player that opened for Ella
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006
